question: Show each store's total revenue as a percentage of overall revenue
expected_sql: WITH store_rev AS (SELECT s.store_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.list_price * (1 - oi.discount)) AS store_total FROM stores s JOIN orders o ON s.store_id = o.store_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY s.store_name), total_rev AS (SELECT SUM(oi.quantity * oi.list_price * (1 - oi.discount)) AS grand_total FROM order_items oi) SELECT sr.store_name, ROUND(sr.store_total / tr.grand_total * 100, 2) AS revenue_pct FROM store_rev sr, total_rev tr ORDER BY revenue_pct DESC
generated_sql: WITH store_rev AS (SELECT s.store_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.list_price * (1 - oi.discount)) AS store_total FROM stores s JOIN orders o ON s.store_id = o.store_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY s.store_id, s.store_name), total_rev AS (SELECT SUM(oi.quantity * oi.list_price * (1 - oi.discount)) AS overall_total FROM order_items oi) SELECT sr.store_name, ROUND((sr.store_total / tr.overall_total) * 100, 2) AS revenue_pct FROM store_rev sr, total_rev tr ORDER BY revenue_pct DESC

graph_state.finalSql: WITH store_rev AS (SELECT s.store_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.list_price * (1 - oi.discount)) AS store_total FROM stores s JOIN orders o ON s.store_id = o.store_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY s.store_id, s.store_name), total_rev AS (SELECT SUM(oi.quantity * oi.list_price * (1 - oi.discount)) AS overall_total FROM order_items oi) SELECT sr.store_name, ROUND((sr.store_total / tr.overall_total) * 100, 2) AS revenue_pct FROM store_rev sr, total_rev tr ORDER BY revenue_pct DESC

question: Find customers who have spent more than the average customer total spending
expected_sql: SELECT c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.list_price * (1 - oi.discount)) AS total_spent FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name HAVING total_spent > (SELECT AVG(customer_total) FROM (SELECT SUM(oi2.quantity * oi2.list_price * (1 - oi2.discount)) AS customer_total FROM orders o2 JOIN order_items oi2 ON o2.order_id = oi2.order_id GROUP BY o2.customer_id) sub) ORDER BY total_spent DESC
generated_sql: WITH customer_spending AS (SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.list_price * (1 - oi.discount)) AS total_spent FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name) SELECT first_name, last_name, total_spent FROM customer_spending WHERE total_spent > (SELECT AVG(total_spent) FROM customer_spending) ORDER BY total_spent DESC

graph_state.finalSql: WITH customer_spending AS (SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.list_price * (1 - oi.discount)) AS total_spent FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name) SELECT first_name, last_name, total_spent FROM customer_spending WHERE total_spent > (SELECT AVG(total_spent) FROM customer_spending) ORDER BY total_spent DESC

question: Show the cheapest product in each category
expected_sql: SELECT c.category_name, p.product_name, p.list_price FROM products p JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id JOIN (SELECT category_id, MIN(list_price) AS min_price FROM products GROUP BY category_id) mn ON p.category_id = mn.category_id AND p.list_price = mn.min_price ORDER BY p.list_price ASC
generated_sql: SELECT c.category_name, p.product_name, p.list_price FROM products p JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id JOIN (SELECT category_id, MIN(list_price) AS min_price FROM products GROUP BY category_id) mn ON p.category_id = mn.category_id AND p.list_price = mn.min_price ORDER BY p.list_price ASC

graph_state.finalSql: SELECT c.category_name, p.product_name, p.list_price FROM products p JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id JOIN (SELECT category_id, MIN(list_price) AS min_price FROM products GROUP BY category_id) mn ON p.category_id = mn.category_id AND p.list_price = mn.min_price ORDER BY p.list_price ASC

question: Find staff members who have handled orders for more than 200 unique customers
expected_sql: SELECT s.first_name, s.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) AS unique_customers FROM staffs s JOIN orders o ON s.staff_id = o.staff_id GROUP BY s.staff_id, s.first_name, s.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) > 200 ORDER BY unique_customers DESC
generated_sql: SELECT s.staff_id, s.first_name, s.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) AS unique_customers FROM staffs s JOIN orders o ON s.staff_id = o.staff_id GROUP BY s.staff_id, s.first_name, s.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) > 200

graph_state.finalSql: SELECT s.staff_id, s.first_name, s.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) AS unique_customers FROM staffs s JOIN orders o ON s.staff_id = o.staff_id GROUP BY s.staff_id, s.first_name, s.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) > 200

question: What is the total quantity sold per brand?
expected_sql: SELECT b.brand_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity_sold FROM brands b JOIN products p ON b.brand_id = p.brand_id JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY b.brand_id, b.brand_name ORDER BY total_quantity_sold DESC
generated_sql: SELECT b.brand_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN brands b ON p.brand_id = b.brand_id GROUP BY b.brand_name ORDER BY total_quantity DESC

graph_state.finalSql: SELECT b.brand_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN brands b ON p.brand_id = b.brand_id GROUP BY b.brand_name ORDER BY total_quantity DESC

question: Which category has the highest average revenue per product?
expected_sql: SELECT c.category_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.list_price * (1 - oi.discount)) / COUNT(DISTINCT p.product_id) AS revenue_per_product FROM categories c JOIN products p ON c.category_id = p.category_id JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY c.category_name ORDER BY revenue_per_product DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql: SELECT c.category_name, AVG(product_revenue) AS avg_revenue_per_product FROM categories c JOIN products p ON c.category_id = p.category_id JOIN (SELECT product_id, SUM(quantity * list_price * (1 - discount)) AS product_revenue FROM order_items GROUP BY product_id) AS product_revenues ON p.product_id = product_revenues.product_id GROUP BY c.category_id, c.category_name ORDER BY avg_revenue_per_product DESC LIMIT 1

graph_state.finalSql: SELECT c.category_name, AVG(product_revenue) AS avg_revenue_per_product FROM categories c JOIN products p ON c.category_id = p.category_id JOIN (SELECT product_id, SUM(quantity * list_price * (1 - discount)) AS product_revenue FROM order_items GROUP BY product_id) AS product_revenues ON p.product_id = product_revenues.product_id GROUP BY c.category_id, c.category_name ORDER BY avg_revenue_per_product DESC LIMIT 1
